steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what both authors would agree on. Text 1 says Impressionists chose painted frames to distinguish themselves from conventional gold Salon frames — a deliberate stylistic decision. Text 2 says those Impressionists who used painted frames chose frame colors to harmonize with the painting's colors — again, a deliberate aesthetic decision. The point of agreement is that frame selection was intentional.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Many Impressionist painters were intentional about the frames they selected for their works.": Both texts support this — Text 1 through contrast with Salon norms, Text 2 through aesthetic harmony. ✓
  • "Degas's preferred framing style was different from that of most Impressionist painters.": Text 1 says Degas used painted frames like many Impressionist painters — not differently from them. ✗
  • "The colors in an Impressionist painting were often chosen to complement the colors of the frame it would be placed in.": Text 2 says the frame was chosen to harmonize with the painting — not the reverse. This flips the direction. ✗
  • "Gold frames were considered especially desirable by those who purchased works from Impressionist painters.": Neither text says buyers preferred gold frames; Text 2 says gold could distract from the work. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
